content.text:
Here are some of the most relevant recent posts on X discussing AI-powered tools and techniques for web scraping, research article extraction, search automation, and related topics like agentic retrieval systems.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018627541104558189)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019061395342622947)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017428603684982973)

### Key Tools and Projects Mentioned:
- **Desearch.ai**: An AI-powered search and scraping API that's 16x more affordable, designed for devs and AI agents, emphasizing high-quality ranking and real-time web scraping for news/dynamic content.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018627541104558189)
- **AnyCrawl**: An open-source LLM-native web scraping tool (2.5k stars) where you describe what you want in natural language, and AI extracts structured data without needing selectors. Contrasts with traditional anti-bot methods.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018912401262014676)
- **ScrapeGraphAI**: LLM-driven web scraping suite for extracting structured data from any website/HTML, now integrated with agentic payment APIs for broader AI agent use.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017428603684982973)
- **SerpApi**: Real-time APIs for scraping Google search results (including a new "Light Search" version for fast critical data extraction), handling proxies/CAPTCHAs, and delivering JSON. Useful for search engine scraping without hassle.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2019169119547859017)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018226399547633742)
- **A-RAG (Agentic RAG)**: New research on hierarchical retrieval agents using tools like keyword_search, semantic_search, and chunk_read. Lets LLMs decide retrieval strategies dynamically, outperforming baselines on multi-hop QA benchmarks (e.g., 94.5% on HotpotQA with GPT-5-mini).[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019061395342622947)

### Research-Focused AI Workflows:
- Tools like Perplexity (Academic mode + Deep Research), NotebookLM (for lit reviews/tables/audio from PDFs/URLs), and others for efficient paper discovery/analysis without manual tab overload.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2017591194826440879)
- "Chat with Research Papers" AI: Paste sections for explanations/inferences.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018602867444363633)
- Literature mapping tools: Elicit, ResearchRabbit, ConnectedPapers, Litmaps, Scite.ai for finding topics/gaps.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2018609923492598268)

Other mentions include data scraping for rural sustainability gaps[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2019004097953767493) and general web/data scraping automation.

These posts highlight a shift toward LLM-native, agentic approaches over traditional scraping. Always check site ToS and legal guidelines for usage. If you need details on a specific post/thread or images/videos viewed, let me know!
